paper_evidence.summary: Patient 1 with BRAF V600E mutated multiple myeloma showed initial response to vemurafenib with pain reduction and paraprotein decrease within 4 days, but progressed after 7 weeks with CNS involvement
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: IgG κ fell from 2.5 to 1.3 g/dL and κ free light chain fell from 41 to 7 mg/dL within 1 month

alignment.notes: Both AI and GT accurately captured the case details, though AI provided more granular quantitative response data

paper_evidence.summary: Patient 2 with BRAF V600E mutated multiple myeloma showed dramatic response to vemurafenib with 90% reduction in κ light chains within 1 month and near-resolution of lesions at 2 months
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: 90% decrease in κ free light chain level within 1 month

alignment.notes: Both AI and GT accurately captured the case details, with AI providing more specific response metrics

paper_evidence.summary: BRAF V600E mutations in multiple myeloma are associated with aggressive phenotype, extramedullary disease, and short duration of response to conventional therapy
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: 4 of 7 patients with extramedullary disease in referenced study

alignment.notes: AI correctly identified prognostic significance that was missing from GT

ai_improvements_over_gt.paper_support: Discussion section clearly states prognostic significance of BRAF V600E in multiple myeloma with supporting data from referenced study

overall_judgement.summary: The AI extraction demonstrated high accuracy in capturing both the predictive evidence from the two patient cases and the prognostic significance of BRAF V600E in multiple myeloma. The AI provided more comprehensive extraction than the ground truth by including the prognostic evidence and more detailed quantitative response data.
strengths: Accurate capture of clinical response details for both patients
Recognition of prognostic significance missing from ground truth
Clear differentiation between predictive and prognostic evidence types
weaknesses: Some clinical trial IDs mentioned may need verification
Safety profile information included but not directly supported by paper text
